title: Bridge Essay: Monitoring Processes and Their Neuronal Correlates as the Basis of Auditory Verb

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Language/Mantra bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Monitoring Processes and Their Neuronal Correlates as the Basis of Auditory Verbal Hallucinations in a Non-clinical Sample.** — Frontiers in psychiatry (2021). PMID: 34707515. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyt.2021.644052. Score: 42. Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) are acharacteristic symptom of psychosis. An influential cognitive model accounting for the mechanisms in the generation of AVHs describes adefective monitoring of inner speech, leading to the misidentification of internally generated thoughts as externally generated events. In this study, we utilized an inner speech paradigm during asimultaneous measurement with functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) and functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI)
